mcqs.question: What is the time complexity of inserting a node at the beginning of a singly linked list?
mcqs.explanation: Inserting at the beginning is O(1) because you only need to create a new node, set its next pointer to the current head, and update the head pointer.

mcqs.question: To insert a node at the end of a singly linked list, you must:

options.text: Traverse the entire list to find the last node
Delete the current last node first

mcqs.explanation: To insert at the end, you must traverse the entire list to find the last node (whose next is NULL), then set its next pointer to the new node. This takes O(n) time.

mcqs.question: When deleting a node from the beginning of a linked list, what must be done?

options.text: Save the head pointer, update head to head->next, and free the old head
Traverse the list to find the first element
Shift all elements one position left

mcqs.explanation: To delete from the beginning: save the current head in a temp variable, update head to point to head->next, then free the temp node.

mcqs.question: The time complexity of searching for an element in a singly linked list is:
mcqs.explanation: Searching in a singly linked list requires sequential traversal from the head, checking each node, which takes O(n) time in the worst case.

mcqs.question: To reverse a singly linked list iteratively, how many pointers are typically used?
mcqs.explanation: Reversing a linked list iteratively uses three pointers: prev (initially NULL), current (starts at head), and next (to save the next node before changing links).

mcqs.question: What happens if you try to delete a node from an empty linked list?

options.text: Nothing happens if underflow is handled
The head points to a random address

mcqs.explanation: A well-implemented delete function checks if the list is empty (head == NULL) before attempting deletion, preventing errors.

mcqs.question: What is the time complexity of counting the number of nodes in a singly linked list?
mcqs.explanation: Counting nodes requires traversing the entire list from head to NULL, visiting each node once, which takes O(n) time.
